chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
MEDICAL RETRIEVAL-AUGMENTED
GENERATION SYSTEM FOR
HEALTHCARE KNOWLEDGE ACCESS

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
INTRODUCTION
The exponential growth of medical data has made it
challenging to access relevant and reliable healthcare
information efficiently.
Traditional search systems lack contextual
understanding, while standalone AI models may
generate unverified or inaccurate responses.
To address this, we propose a Medical Retrieval-
Augmented Generation (RAG) system that combines
semantic search with domain-specific AI to deliver
accurate, evidence-based medical insights.
Traditional search systems like keyword search often
return large volumes of irrelevant information, which
makes it difficult for doctors to find precise answers
quickly.
Modern Large Language Models (LLMs) can generate
natural language responses but sometimes produce
hallucinations (incorrect information) because they rely
only

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
rge Language Models (LLMs) can generate
natural language responses but sometimes produce
hallucinations (incorrect information) because they rely
only on their training data.
To solve this issue, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
is used. RAG combines information retrieval and generative
AI to produce accurate, evidence-based medical answers.

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
PROBLEM STATEMENT
 Information Overload
Fragmented Data
Sources
The healthcare domain is overwhelmed with massive volumes of medical research, clinical data, and
rapidly evolving knowledge, making it increasingly difficult to quickly access relevant, reliable, and up-
to-date information.
Traditional search engines return unstructured and time-consuming results, requiring significant effort
to filter and interpret useful insights. At the same time, generative AI models often produce
hallucinated or non-verified medical responses, which can be risky in critical healthcare scenarios.
This creates a significant gap where users lack an intelligent system that can efficiently retrieve trusted
medical knowledge and generate accurate, context-aware, and evidence-based insights in real

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
n intelligent system that can efficiently retrieve trusted
medical knowledge and generate accurate, context-aware, and evidence-based insights in real time,
ensuring both reliability and usability.

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
PROPOSED SOLUTION
We propose MedIntel RAG, an advanced Medical Retrieval-
Augmented Generation System designed to provide accurate,
evidence-based medical knowledge retrieval and explanation.
The system integrates biomedical language models with
vector-based semantic search to ensure that every generated
response is grounded in real medical literature.
Key Capabilities
• Retrieve relevant medical research papers using semantic search
 • Generate context-aware explanations using BioMistral 7B
 • Reduce AI hallucinations by grounding responses in verified medical knowledge
 • Provide fast and intelligent access to biomedical information
How the System Works
User Query → Semantic Search → Medical Knowledge Retrieval → AI-Powered Explanation
Key Innovation
Unlike traditional AI chatbots, our

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
he System Works
User Query → Semantic Search → Medical Knowledge Retrieval → AI-Powered Explanation
Key Innovation
Unlike traditional AI chatbots, our system first retrieves
 relevant medical evidence and then generates answers based on that evidence,
 ensuring higher reliability and transparency.

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE
Step 1: Medical Data
Collection
Step 2: Data Preprocessing
Medical knowledge is collected from
trusted sources such as:
PubMed
WHO guidelines
Medical textbooks
Research journals
Clinical datasets
The collected documents are
prepared for processing:
Cleaning text
Removing noise
Splitting documents into smaller
chunks
This improves retrieval accuracy.
Step 3: Embedding
Generation
Text data is converted into vector
embeddings using embedding models.
Embedding models represent text in
numerical form so AI systems can
compare similarity between queries and
documents.
Sentence Transformers
OpenAI embeddings
BioBERT
Step 4: Vector Database
Embeddings are stored in a vector
database.
FAISS
Pinecone
Chroma
Weaviate
These databases allow fast similarity
search.
Step 5: Retrieval

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
or Database
Embeddings are stored in a vector
database.
FAISS
Pinecone
Chroma
Weaviate
These databases allow fast similarity
search.
Step 5: Retrieval Module
When a user asks a question:
1.The query is converted into an
embedding.
2.The vector database searches for
similar documents.
3.The most relevant medical
knowledge is retrieved.
The retrieved documents are passed to
an LLM such as:
GPT
LLaMA
Med-PaLM
The LLM generates an answer based on
the retrieved context.
This ensures the answer is fact-based
and reliable.
Step 6: Large Language
Model (LLM)

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
Impacts Benefits of
Medical RAG Systems
1. Accurate Medical Information
Answers are generated using verified medical sources.
2. Reduced AI Hallucinations
The model relies on retrieved documents rather than only training
data.
3. Faster Knowledge Access
Doctors can quickly find medical information during clinical practice.
4. Evidence-Based Medicine
Responses are grounded in scientific research.
5. Support for Medical Education
Medical students can use the system for learning and research.

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
Future Scope
The system can be extended into a clinical decision support tool to assist doctors with real-time, evidence-
based recommendations.
• Integration with real-time hospital data and electronic health records (EHRs) can enable personalized
medical insights.
• Expansion to include more trusted datasets such as World Health Organization guidelines and global
clinical research for broader coverage.Development of a multilingual interface to make medical knowledge
accessible to users in regional languages.
• Addition of voice-based interaction to support hands-free usage for healthcare professionals and
patients.
• Incorporation of explainable AI features, showing sources and reasoning behind each generated response.
• Scaling the system into a mobile or web application for widespread

chunks.docName: Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Healthcare Knowledge Access.pdf
chunks.text:
inable AI features, showing sources and reasoning behind each generated response.
• Scaling the system into a mobile or web application for widespread public and professional use.
• Integration with wearable devices for real-time health monitoring and intelligent suggestio
